title: review-bot issue sweep (#11-#13)
description:
Implement every issue currently open on olli/review-bot: #11, #12, and #13. Restore repo-audit checkout lifecycle correctness and harden the credential-free Unix-socket client so connection failures are precise, traceback-free, and conservative about unknown outcomes. Preserve established PR review, issue triage, repo-audit, protocol, and output behavior outside the referenced fixes.

Acceptance: every tests/test_*.py script exits 0; the Nix package builds; in-use integration exercises the actual client against successful and abruptly failing fake Unix-socket peers and exercises repo-audit with realistic and degenerate local Git trees plus fake Forgejo/engine dependencies. The eventual PR must include Closes #11, Closes #12, and Closes #13.

title: Harden client diagnostics and indeterminate connection-loss handling (#12, #13)
description:
Closes #12, #13.

Update client.py and focused client protocol tests. Replace every user-facing reference to the nonexistent review-bot.socket unit with review-bot-review.socket. Keep missing-socket and refused-connection hints focused on the real unit and REVIEW_BOT_SOCKET. Give PermissionError its own concise diagnostic naming review-bot-review.socket, the review-bot-client supplementary group, and the possibility that a login/session or long-running user manager must be restarted after group changes. All failures must remain traceback-free and nonzero.

Handle abrupt connection loss during send, shutdown, and response reading, including ConnectionResetError, BrokenPipeError, and equivalent supported-runtime errors. A complete final result remains authoritative: preserve ok:true output/exit behavior even if the peer then resets, and preserve ok:false error/exit behavior. If transport is lost or clean EOF occurs before a final result, exit nonzero with one concise diagnostic stating that the service connection was lost, the review outcome is unknown and may already have posted, and the caller must inspect the target or service journal before retrying. Never retry automatically or infer success. Preserve malformed/unknown NDJSON forward compatibility unless it prevents receiving the result.

Add stdlib-only deterministic tests with no live systemd, Forgejo, or engine. Test exact exit status and full stdout/stderr for FileNotFoundError, ConnectionRefusedError, and PermissionError. Use an actual client subprocess plus a fake local Unix-socket peer for reset before response, log then reset, complete success then close/reset, explicit ok:false, clean EOF without result, and clean success. Include a successful service-path assertion that a complete final result is emitted and the service path exits 0; fix a repository-local cleanup/shutdown defect only if the test exposes one.

Acceptance: no client text names review-bot.socket; all specified diagnostics and protocol cases pass; the running client integration proves observable output and exit semantics for realistic success and degenerate abrupt-loss data.

title: Restore repo-audit Checkout lifecycle and cleanup (#11)
description:
Closes #11.

Update do_repo_audit() in review.py for the current prepare_head_checkout() contract: receive (Checkout, head_sha), enter the Checkout context, use checkout.wt as the only repository path, and keep every checkout-dependent operation inside the context. This includes convention discovery and all generation, verification, and synthesis engine calls. Ensure context exit and private-worktree/temp-ref cleanup occur after successful dry-run, print-only, and normal issue-creation paths and after injected generation or verification failures. Preserve repo-audit rendering, output format, issue deduplication, create-issue behavior, PR review, and issue triage. Do not change the Checkout or prepare_head_checkout APIs.

Replace the obsolete raw-path test double in tests/test_repo_audit.py with a realistic context-manager double exposing a distinct .wt path and recording entry/exit. Tests must fail if the Checkout object itself reaches filesystem or subprocess APIs, prove entry precedes checkout-dependent work, prove engine cwd is .wt, and prove __exit__ on success and failure while retaining dry-run, print-only, deduplication, and create-issue coverage. Exercise the repo-audit pipeline with a realistic local Git tree containing source/convention content and a degenerate minimal tree, using only fake Forgejo and engine dependencies; assert observable rendered/output behavior.

Acceptance: repo mode never treats Checkout as a path, always cleans it up on every specified path, all existing behavior remains intact, and the running pipeline integration succeeds without live Forgejo or a real review harness.
